skill.name: complexity-analyzer
skill.description: Measure and report code complexity metrics with actionable recommendations.
skill.summary: Measure and report code complexity metrics with actionable recommendations.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and no executable behavior was confirmed. The external command detections are Markdown code fences and usage examples. The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance detections have no supporting evidence in the cited lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 70, 77, and 81. These lines are Markdown code fences and usage or report examples, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside documentation fences and contain sample skill invocations or report formatting. No script, command runner, or user input execution path exists in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 3. The cited line is the skill description and does not reference any cryptographic algorithm or hashing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text only describes measuring code complexity metrics. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md line 166. The cited line is a best practice about avoiding deep nesting, not a request to inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is documentation about code structure and contains no command, filesystem enumeration, environment probing, or host discovery instruction.

content.user_title: Analyze Code Complexity and Refactoring Risk
content.value_statement: Complex code hides defects, slows reviews, and makes maintenance harder. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to measure complexity and recommend focused refactoring.
actual_capabilities: Measures cyclomatic complexity, cognitive complexity, lines of code, nesting depth, parameters, and return points.
Ranks functions and files by complexity to identify the highest maintenance risk.
Applies clear thresholds for low, moderate, high, and very high complexity.
Produces report sections with summaries, hotspot lists, distributions, and recommendations.
Suggests language-specific tooling such as ESLint, radon, Checkstyle, gocyclo, flog, and reek.
limitations: It provides analysis guidance and does not include an executable analyzer script.
Metric accuracy depends on the AI tool, parser support, and the code context supplied.
It does not automatically modify source code or enforce quality gates.
Duplicate code detection may be approximate without repository-wide tooling.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Refactoring Work
use_cases.description: Find the functions that create the greatest maintenance cost before starting a cleanup sprint.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Check whether a change adds risky branching, long functions, deep nesting, or too many parameters.

use_cases.title: Improve Legacy Modules
use_cases.description: Map complex files and choose smaller refactoring steps that reduce risk over time.

prompt_templates.title: Basic File Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when checking one file for obvious complexity issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the complexity-analyzer skill on this file. Report the most complex functions, their scores, and the first three refactoring opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Directory Hotspot Scan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when planning work across a source directory.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the complexity-analyzer skill on the src directory. Rank the top complexity hotspots and explain which files need review first.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Risk Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when reviewing a proposed change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the complexity-analyzer skill on this diff. Identify new complexity, changed thresholds, and refactoring suggestions for the review.

prompt_templates.title: Refactoring Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing a structured cleanup plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the complexity-analyzer skill to create a refactoring plan for these high complexity functions. Include priority, expected benefit, and test focus.

output_examples.input: A payment processor file with long validation and transaction logic.
output_examples.output: The report highlights one very high complexity function, notes deep nesting and many branches, and recommends extracting validation, fee calculation, and error handling.

output_examples.input: A pull request that adds several conditionals to an existing service method.
output_examples.output: The review flags increased cyclomatic complexity, explains the new decision paths, and suggests guard clauses or smaller helper functions.

output_examples.input: A legacy reports directory with many files and mixed coding styles.
output_examples.output: The summary ranks the most complex files, groups functions by risk level, and proposes a staged cleanup plan.

best_practices: Provide the full function or file when asking for metric estimates.
Combine complexity results with test coverage and recent change frequency.
Use the highest risk functions to guide small, test-backed refactoring steps.
anti_patterns: Treating one metric as the only reason to rewrite working code.
Ignoring business rules that make some complexity unavoidable.
Requesting precise repository-wide metrics without giving repository context or tool output.

faq.question: Does this skill run a complexity scanner?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis instructions and report structure for supported AI tools.

faq.question: Which metrics does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers cyclomatic complexity, cognitive complexity, lines of code, maintainability, nesting depth, parameters, and related function metrics.

faq.question: Can it analyze multiple languages?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, and Ruby tooling.

faq.question: Can it recommend refactoring steps?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests actions such as extracting methods, reducing nesting, and grouping related parameters.

faq.question: Is it suitable for pull request review?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare changes against complexity thresholds and identify risky additions.

faq.question: Does it replace automated CI checks?
faq.answer: No. Use it with automated tools when exact metrics or enforceable gates are required.
